pr.title: matched the final function in fighter.c that was temp moved to ftanim.s
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Matched the previously stubborn final fighter.c function that had been temporarily parked in asm/melee/ft/ftanim.s. The PR removed the asm implementation of func_8006DABC, reimplemented it in C as Fighter_Unload_8006DABC, updated call sites and declarations to the new symbol, and renamed the callback table lbl_803C125C to ft_OnUserDataRemove. The author explicitly noted the match required a strange uninitialized-pointer pattern and produces a compiler warning, but the function and DOL matched.
what_changed: Removed the assembly body for func_8006DABC from asm/melee/ft/ftanim.s, eliminating the temporary asm fallback for this fighter.c function.
Added a C implementation named Fighter_Unload_8006DABC in src/melee/ft/fighter.c.
Updated Fighter_80068E98 to pass Fighter_Unload_8006DABC to GObj_InitUserData instead of func_8006DABC.
Updated asm references in code_800877F8.s from func_8006DABC to Fighter_Unload_8006DABC so remaining asm call sites use the new symbol name.
Renamed the callback table symbol lbl_803C125C to ft_OnUserDataRemove in ftdata.s and variables.h.
Changed fighter.h declaration from void func_8006DABC(HSD_GObj* fighterObj) to void Fighter_Unload_8006DABC(Fighter* fighter).

Introduced an inline helper sub_func that accesses ft_OnUserDataRemove using an intentionally uninitialized Fighter pointer, then returns &lbl_80458FD0, matching the original register/codegen behavior according to the PR body and source comment.
smart_moves: Kept the cleanup/unload behavior in C while preserving exact codegen through a small inline helper rather than leaving the function in asm.
Renamed the anonymous callback array lbl_803C125C to ft_OnUserDataRemove, reflecting its use as a per-character callback invoked during userdata removal/unload.
Named func_8006DABC as Fighter_Unload_8006DABC, which documents the lifecycle role better than a raw address name and aligns with nearby fighter-prefixed names.
Updated all visible references, including asm references and header declarations, to keep the new symbol linkable across mixed C/asm code.
Documented the weird matching tactic directly in the source comment with the decomp.me scratch link and warning caveat.
decomp_lessons: A matched function may require preserving undefined-looking source patterns if they reproduce original compiler register allocation; here the author used an uninitialized Fighter* in an inline helper and accepted a compiler warning.
When moving a function from temporary asm back to C, update both C call sites and any remaining asm references to the new symbol name.
Callback table renames should be evidence-driven by observed behavior: the table at lbl_803C125C is invoked during fighter unload/userdata removal before teardown, supporting ft_OnUserDataRemove.
Object allocator arrays can appear in C as indexed HSD_ObjFree calls using byte-offset-derived indices such as objAllocData[(0xDC / 40)], objAllocData[(0x58 / 40)], and objAllocData[(0x2C / 40)].
For difficult matches, isolating the register-sensitive part into an inline helper can be a viable tactic when straightforward code does not match.
naming_conventions: Raw address-like fighter functions may be renamed with a Fighter_* prefix plus an inferred lifecycle role while retaining the address suffix, as in func_8006DABC -> Fighter_Unload_8006DABC.
Per-character callback arrays use ft_On* names when their lifecycle purpose is known, as in lbl_803C125C -> ft_OnUserDataRemove.
Header prototypes should use the real domain type once known; this PR changes the unload function argument to Fighter* rather than HSD_GObj*.

Existing field names such as x2D8_specialAttributes2, x890_cameraBox, x20A0_accessory, x8AC_animSkeleton, x5E8_fighterBones, and x221F_flag.bits.b4 were reused in the C implementation rather than introducing new speculative names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted an 83-line asm implementation from ftanim.s after producing matching C in fighter.c.
Used an inline sub_func returning HSD_ObjAllocData* to force codegen around loading &lbl_80458FD0 and invoking the ft_OnUserDataRemove callback.
Used an intentionally uninitialized local Fighter* named uninitalized_fighter to match the original function, with the author noting this causes a compiler warning.

Matched the original cleanup sequence: optional per-character remove callback, fighter-related cleanup calls, conditional HSD_JObjRemoveAll on x20A0_accessory, multiple HSD_ObjFree calls via lbl_80458FD0-derived allocator entries, and final free of the Fighter.
Adjusted remaining asm label references so branches/calls targeting the old func_8006DABC symbol now target Fighter_Unload_8006DABC.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the supplied PR slice.
The PR body notes that the function and DOL match but the C source triggers a compiler warning about an uninitialized variable.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that remove or refactor the uninitialized pointer workaround in Fighter_Unload_8006DABC.
Search for ft_OnUserDataRemove usages to confirm the callback table semantics and whether all entries remain per-character callbacks.
Search for lbl_80458FD0 allocator indexing patterns to improve struct typing for fighter object allocation data.
Search for later review or cleanup of the typo-like local name uninitalized_fighter.
